name: Resampler Speaker
description: The `resampler` speaker platform allows you to convert the sample rate of an audio stream and output it to another [speaker component](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/).

config_entries.label: Audio DAC
config_entries.description: The [audio DAC](https://esphome.io/components/audio_dac/) to use for volume control.

config_entries.label: Output Speaker
config_entries.description: The [speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) to output the resampled audio.

config_entries.label: Bits Per Sample
config_entries.description: The audio sample bit depth after resampling. Defaults to `passthrough`, where the bit depth matches the input audio.

config_entries.label: Buffer Duration
config_entries.description: The duration of the internal ring buffer. Larger values may reduce stuttering but use more memory. Defaults to `500ms`.

config_entries.label: Filters
config_entries.description: The number of windowed sinc interpolation filters to use. Must be between `2` and `1024`. Defaults to `16`.

config_entries.label: Sample Rate
config_entries.description: Sample rate to convert to. Must be between `8000` and `48000`. Defaults to the output speaker's sample rate.

config_entries.label: Taps
config_entries.description: The number of taps per windowed sinc interpolation filter. Must between `16` and `128` and divisible by 4. Defaults to `16`.

config_entries.label: Task Stack In PSRAM
config_entries.description: Run the audio tasks in external memory. Defaults to `false`.
